$ curl -fsSL https://flint.sh/install | sh

  flint v1.4.2 — fast linter for shell scripts

  A zero-dependency linter for POSIX shell scripts. It catches unquoted
  variables, missing error checks, and bashisms before they reach prod.
  Release notes: https://blog.flint.sh/releases/v1-4-2
  Source and issue tracker: https://github.com/flint-sh/flint

Installed files:
  binary        /usr/local/bin/flint
  man page      /usr/local/share/man/man1/flint.1
  completions   /usr/local/share/zsh/site-functions/_flint

Runtime directories:
  config        ~/.config/flint/flint.toml
  system config /etc/flint/defaults.toml
  cache         ~/.cache/flint/
  log           ~/.local/share/flint/flint.log

Next steps:
  1. Run the setup wizard to create your user config:
       flint init --config ~/.config/flint/flint.toml --profile strict
     Edit ~/.config/flint/flint.toml afterwards to tune severity levels.
  2. Lint a script to verify the install:
       flint check /usr/local/bin/flint --verbose
  3. Add a pre-commit hook:
       flint hook install --hook pre-commit

Editor integration (Neovim, Helix, VS Code):
  https://docs.flint.sh/editor-integration

To update, re-run the install command:
  curl -fsSL https://flint.sh/install | sh

To uninstall (keeps config and cache):
  flint uninstall

For a full removal including ~/.config/flint/flint.toml and ~/.cache/flint/:
  flint uninstall --purge
